item.title: 美国对伊朗发动新一轮空袭，德黑兰向三个海湾阿拉伯国家进行还击
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United States launched new airstrikes hitting Iran early Thursday, sparking retaliatory Iranian fire targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar in crossfire that again threatened an interim deal aimed at findinga way to end the wargripping the Persian Gulf.
The strikes came hours after U.S. President Donald Trump said recent Iranian attacks on ships inthe Strait of Hormuzsignaled the end ofthe fragile ceasefire. The U.S. military hit a variety of military sites and port facilities early Wednesday followingIran’s targeting of several merchant vesselsoff the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.
But Thursday’s attacks appeared bigger all around. There was no immediate word of damage in the three Gulf Arab countries. Kuwait’s military said it was actively intercepting incoming drones and missiles.
Military officials said in a social media post that the latest strikes were intended to “further degrade” Iran’s ability “to threaten freedom of navigation” in the strait, through which a fifth of the world’s traded oil and natural gas passed beforethe war beganwith U.S. and Israeli attacks on Feb. 28.
Iranian state media reported explosions in several locations, including Bushehr, home toIran’s nuclear power plant complex, and the southern port cities of Chabahar, Konarak, Bandar Abbas and Sirik.
After leavinga NATO summitin Turkey, Trump posted several videos on his social media site of what he said were explosions in Iran and issued another warning to the Islamic Republic.
“This is in retribution for yesterday’s bombing of ships by Iran. If it happens again, it will get much worse!” Trump wrote.
Trump had said earlier in the day that the latest back-and-forth fighting would not result in “long-term” military action.
The U.S. military has attacked Iran after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Jon Gambrell with The Associated Press in Dubai explains how the assault Wednesday came as part of an American effort that also revoked the Islamic Republic’s ability to openly sell crude oil in the world market.
U.S. President Donald Trump meets with Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon)
“Anything that happens is going to happen very fast,” Trump said, though he also suggested the U.S. military might “just finish the job.”
Trump also renewedhis past threatsto hit Iran’s civilian infrastructure, including electric plants and desalinization plants, and to seize theoil-production hub of Kharg Island.
After three tankers were hit Tuesday, the U.S. launched strikes on Iran, and Iranian forces retaliated by targeting American military sites in the Persian Gulf.
Iran has asserted that the interim ceasefire deal gives it the right to manage traffic through the strait.Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, a key negotiator in talks seeking a permanent end to the war, was defiant in a post on X: “The era of bullying and extortion is over. It leads nowhere. We don’t fold.”
Trump fueled concerns that the war could restart by saying the interim agreement to pause fighting was “over,” although he added that he would allow negotiations to continue.
Attacks have repeatedly threatened the shaky ceasefire, but Trump’s comments added new uncertainty, and oil pricesshot upafter he spoke. A renewed conflict could engulf the wider Middle East and would likely again halt energy shipments through the strait.
“For me, I think it’s over,” Trump said when asked about the status of the ceasefire. He added that U.S. representatives can continue negotiations, but he cast doubt on the outcome. “They can talk, but I think they’re wasting their time,” he said.
Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi, also a top negotiator, retorted on X that Trump’s remarks “are not a sign of power but an admission of the failure” of U.S. policy toward Iran.
Trump has made other threats to seize Kharg Island,including last month, when he also questioned whether the U.S. “has the stomach for it.” Some 90% of Iranian oil exports pass throughthe island.
The new attacks on ships in the strait, despite the negotiations, could reflect a divide among Iran’s leadership. Hard-liners seek lasting control over the waterway, which is a globally important conduit for fuel shipments and has become a critical lever in confronting the West. Pragmatists want a permanent peace deal to lift international sanctions and providedesperately neededeconomic relief.
Negotiations to reach a final deal had been due to start after the funeral for Iran’sSupreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed Feb. 28 in the war’s first moments. The funeral, which ends Thursday, was supposed to be a period of lower tensions.
The talks are meant to focus on the toughest matters, including fully reopening the strait and rolling backTehran’s disputed nuclear program.
A group of people stands in shallow water as a cargo ship appears anchored in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran, Tuesday, June 30, 2026. (Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA via AP)
On Tuesday, the U.S. military’s Central Command said American forces hit Iranian targets including air-defense systems, radars and over 60 small boats used by Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard.
Those boats have been key to threatening ships in the strait. Iran’s ability to bring shipping in the waterway to a near halt during the war proved its greatest strategic advantage.
After the Iranian strikes on shipping, the U.S. revoked a license that — for the first time in years — had allowed Iran to conduct oil sales openly in U.S. dollars, as part of the interim deal.
Iran and the United States agreedas part of the interim dealto allow ships to pass through the strait without paying charges for 60 days. But Tehran has insisted it must control the vessels’ routes and vowed to later charge fees for passage. That would upend decades of practice in the waterway. The ships attacked Tuesday all appeared to be using a route close to Oman’s shore, rather than one ordered by Tehran.
The U.S. and many Gulf Arab statessay they will not agree to Iran charging for passage through the strait.
Women talk in front of a banner with graphic depicting the late Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei kissing head of the late commander of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard expeditionary Quds Force, Gen. Qassem Soleimani, who was killed in a U.S. drone attack in 2020 in Iraq, as they wait for the green light to cross an intersection in downtown Tehran, Iran, Monday, June 29, 2026. (AP Photo/Vahid Salemi)
Mourners gather in prayer during the funeral procession for the late Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei inside the Imam Ali Shrine in Najaf, Iraq, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Anmar Khalil)
Kim reported from Ankara, Turkey. Toropin reported from Washington. Associated Press writers Nasser Karimi in Tehran, Iran; Qassim Abdul-Zahra in Najaf, Iraq; and Collin Binkley in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 特朗普称美国将给予乌克兰生产爱国者防御系统的许可证
item.description:
ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump said at a NATO summit Wednesday in Turkey that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to makePatriot air defense systemsto counter missile attacks from Russia in theirmore than four-year war, a huge coup for Kyiv which has long requested the technology.
Allowing foreign manufacture of Patriots, which the U.S. had resisted, was a turnaround for Trump that mirrored his day at the NATO meeting: Upon arriving, he lashed out at European partners for resisting his efforts totake control of Greenlandand for not supporting his war in Iran. But by day’s end, he described a gathering of unity and “tremendous love,” and praised member nations on their progress in increasing their defense spending.
NATO’s European members plus Canada have scrambled to meet the alliance’s increaseddefense spending targets, which Trump has demanded as the U.S. draws down troops in Europe and insists the continent take more responsibility for its own security.
Trump had reopened old wounds among the32 NATO leadersby insisting again ahead of the summit that the U.S. should control Greenland,a semiautonomous Danish territory. That led Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen to say her country is “ready to defend every inch of NATO including our own territory.”
President Donald Trump has strong words for Iran, while at the NATO summit in Turkey. AP correspondent Donna Warder reports.
Trump also blasted some European countries for refusing to participate in the Iran campaign,singling out Spainas “a terrible partner in NATO” andrenewing his threatsto cut off trade.
But the tone of Trump’s meeting with the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was a break from earlier encounters whichended in acrimony, and Trump praised the Ukrainian leader’s willingness to reach a deal on ending the fighting in Ukraine.
“We’ve actually developed a good relationship. It’s hard to believe,” Trump said during a news conference with Zelenskyy, adding that he believed a deal on ending the war was on the horizon and that the U.S. would “work on some kind of security package” to provide to Ukraine.
Trump said the Ukrainian president has “done an amazing job” and “been very effective” in the war as he pledged to grant Ukraine a license to manufacture the Patriot defense systems.
“We’ll give them the right to make Patriots. We’ll show them how to do it,” Trump said. “I think they can produce them pretty quickly.”
Patriots are expensive, in high demand and take a long time to produce. Zelenskyy has for years been asking for more of them, and more recently for a license so that Ukraine can manufacture its own.
Ahead of the summit, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte praised Trump for theseries of U.S. strikes on Iranovernight, after Tehran struck three merchant shipsin the Strait of Hormuz.
“I think what you did last night was absolutely necessary,” Rutte said to Trump. “It was a very strong response, and I’m with you on this.”
The U.S. strikes, as well as the revoking of a license allowing Iran to sell its oil on global markets, underscored the fragility of an interim deal to end months of fighting.
Trump said of the interim agreement with Iran: “For me, I think it’s over” — but added he will allow talks to continue.
“It’s just a waste of time dealing with them,” he said.
Rutte has dedicated ahuge amount of energyto keeping Trump’s support for NATO and to holding the summit together. On Wednesday, he sought to tamp down the president’s ire by giving him credit for recent increases in defense spending from NATO allies.
“Grab the win. It’s there,” Rutte told Trump on Wednesday.
The NATO chief pointed to countries including Estonia, Latvia, Poland and Denmark that are investing more in defense, but noted that the Trump administration expects “the Europeans and Canadians will equalize their spending with the United States.”
Last month Rutte went to Washington to hail the “Trump Trillion” — the $1.2 trillion that European allies and Canada have added to defense spending since Trump came to power in 2017.
As leaders converged on Ankara, Rutte hosted a “big reveal” event to showcase the many deals planned for the increased spending — much of it to be spent on U.S. companies, creating thousands of jobs for Americans.
At last year’s summit, the allies agreed toinvest 5% of their gross domestic product on defense— 3.5% on their defense budgets and 1.5% on infrastructure so troops and equipment can move faster in times of conflict.
Yet figures released by NATO on Tuesday showed that Slovenia, Belgium, Spain and the Czech Republic have struggled to meet the alliance’s old spending target of 2% of GDP.
The Trump administration wants to see a leaner “NATO 3.0,” with Europe taking responsibility for its own security, including Ukraine, with conventional weapons while America would continue to provide its nuclear umbrella.
The Pentagon has launched asix-month review of U.S. military presencein Europe, leaving allies to seek clarity on just how deeply Trump intends to cut U.S. force numbers.
Zelenskyy made a fresh appeal Tuesday for Ukraine to be allowed to join the alliance, saying Ukrainian armed forces are highly experienced and would only boost NATO’s defense capabilities. Russia is vehemently opposed to that.
Zelenskyy has highlighted Ukraine’s adaptability and its ability to strike deep inside Russia. He said Ukraine’s armed forces are “eliminating” on average 30,000 Russian troops every month.
In a declaration following Wednesday’s summit, NATO leaders pledged to provide Ukraine with $80 billion to help meet its defense needs this year and next, noting “the long-term threat Russia poses to Euro-Atlantic security.”
Concern has been mounting among some countries with borders near Russia that Moscow might be preparing a hybrid attack — a combination of conventional warfare with tactics like cyberattacks — on the continent as Russian President Vladimir Putin struggles tosecure victory in Ukraine.
Trump also met withSyrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa, a former insurgent who led the offensive that unseated autocrat Bashar Assad in December 2024. Despite having once been an al-Qaida fighter, al-Sharaa has won Trump’s backing as he seeks to rebuild Syria and restore its shattered ties with the West.
Later, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in a statement that Trump has told U.S. lawmakers that the U.S. will soon remove Syria as a state sponsor of terrorism as part of a year-long normalization process with the country’s new government.
“Lifting sanctions on Syria will unlock international trade and investment, give Syria a chance to rebuild, and open up a new chapter for the Syrian people,” Rubio said.
In June 2025, Trump signed an executive order ending a number of economic sanctions before revoking the terrorism designation a few weeks later for al-Sharaa, though the designation for Syria remained.
Associated Press journalists Collin Binkley and Michelle L. Price in Washington and Farnoush Amiri at the United Nations contributed.

item.title: Telegram 上的性掠夺者网络成员试图用暗号隐藏他们的罪行
item.description:
BERLIN (AP) — They called themselves the “German driving school for experts,” but prosecutors say the true purpose of theirTelegram chatswas to brag about the women they raped and share tips about how to drug them.
In posts that sometimes included photos and videos of their attacks on unconscious victims, they referred to women as “cars,” sedatives as “fuel” and rape as “driving,” according to court documents. They called their victims “dead pigs.”
Investigators have been poring through several years’ worth of posts in roughly two dozen group chats on the popular messaging app that authorities believe served an online predator network of mainlyChinese mentargeting mostly Chinese women inGermany. Their investigation has already led to the convictions of three alleged inner circle members on rape and other charges. A fourth was convicted Wednesday in Berlin.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
EDITOR’S NOTE: This story includes discussion of sexual violence. If you or someone you know needs help, please call 1-800-656-4673 in the U.S., 116 016 in Germany or 15117905157 in China.
“The perpetrators were characterized by a particular ruthlessness, an objectification of the victims, and the perfidious planning of their crimes,” Frankfurt chief prosecutor Dominik Mies told The Associated Press.
Major details of the investigation remain unknown, at least to the public, including how many attacks and perpetrators have been linked to the GermanTelegram chatsand how the chats, some of which reportedly had tens of thousands of members, could have operated for so long. It’s also unclear if the chats are linked to a ballooning investigation in Europe and the Americas into drug-facilitated sexual assaults by misogynist online communities.
The defendant covers his face as he arrives for the trial in Munich, Germany, Feb. 2, 2026. (Malin Wunderlich/dpa via AP)
Under German privacy laws, prosecutors are limited in what they can say outside the courtroom, documents are restricted and, in the ongoing case in Berlin, members of the public have been forced to leave the courtroom during parts of the trial.
This may be why the investigation into the Telegram group has garnered less attention in Germany than might be expected. But members of the country’s Chinese community, mostly women, have been attending court proceedings to show support for the victims even if they don’t know them.
People walk in the court on the day of the trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
“What makes one really angry is to see that such groups hate women, they have no respect,” said Fu Xiao, who traveled roughly 500 kilometers (310 miles) to Berlin last week to attend the trial. “Women aren’t seen as people.”
In China, state media has covered the cases comprehensively, but wider discussion about the prosecutions on Chinese-language social media like Rednote has beenpartially censored. Certain tags have been more likely to get a post deleted or banned on Rednote, screenshots and searches show. But posts using less direct language havesurvived the censors, including ones that refer to “date rape” or the euphemistic “students studying abroad in Germany.”
China’s Ministry of Public Security and Rednote didn’t respond to requests for comment.
The German cases have drawn comparisons to the attacks onGisèle Pelicot, a French woman who, over the course of nearly a decade,was repeatedly drugged and rapedby her then-husband and strangers he invited to their home. The trial — and Pelicot’s decision to waive her anonymity — prompted a reckoning over rape culture in France and beyond.
“Pelicot is not an isolated case,” Judge Markus Koppenleitner said during a hearing in Munich for one of the Chinese men convicted in the German investigation. “This is not a Chinese or French phenomenon, but one that also exists in Germany and, ultimately, worldwide.”
The defendant covers his face as he arrives for the trial, in Munich, Germany, Feb. 2, 2026. (Malin Wunderlich/dpa via AP)
Similar cases to the “German driving school” investigation have been popping up around the globe. Although authorities haven’t publicly linked them to the German prosecutions, some investigators have cited tips from German authorities and journalists as crucial to their progress.
In Los Angeles, German investigators last year reached out to police about a potential suspect in drug-facilitated sexual assaults. The defendant, a graduate student from China, is accused of drugging and sexually assaulting three women in LA after he allegedly procured the drugs from a Chinese national in Germany.
In the Netherlandslast month, police arrested four men suspected of drugging and sexually abusing women after hearing from authorities in Germany and the U.K. Dutch police said the alleged perpetrators used social media chat groups to disseminate videos showing the abuse and discuss how to drug victims.
And Europol, the European Union’s police agency, last week announced “Project Medusa,” an international operation designed to dismantle online networks that promote drug-facilitated sexual assaults. Law enforcement from Germany and the U.K. are leading the operation, which has already netted 57 arrests.
The German predator network managed to thrive despite clear violations of Telegram’s terms of service, again raising questions about how the platform has been used forcriminal activity.
In 2024, the app’s founderwas arrested in Parisover allegations that the platform was being used for illicit activity, including drug trafficking and the distribution of child sexual abuse images. He denied wrongdoing, blaming surging numbers of Telegram users that he said “caused growing pains that made it easier for criminals to abuse our platform.” The investigation is ongoing.
“Sexual violence is explicitly forbidden by Telegram’s terms of service and such content is routinely removed,” the company said in a statement. “Telegram fulfils all of its legal obligations in relation to such harmful content, including everything set out by” the European Union’s Digital Services Act.
The company didn’t respond to questions about the German cases, including how photos, videos and comments about sexual crimes were posted for years in the app, whether Telegram was aware of the activity and what, if anything, it did to alert the authorities.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
Some of the German Telegram chats date back to at least 2020, court documents show. Attorney Magdalena Gebhard, who represented a victim in a previous Berlin trial that led to a conviction, said there was an inner circle of eight perpetrators but that some of the chat groups had up to 50,000 members.
Police only became aware of the network in 2024 after a man in Frankfurt, referred to by German courts as Dapeng Z., changed his tactics from drugging and sexually abusing female acquaintances to targeting strangers he met online, according to prosecutors.
German police arrested Dapeng Z., whom German and Chinese media have reported is the group’s ringleader, in 2024 in cooperation with Chinese law enforcement, according to the Chinese consulate in Frankfurt and the Beijing News, a state-run media outlet.
He was sentenced in February to 14 years in prison for aggravated rape, attempted murder and other offenses, though he has appealed. His attorneys didn’t respond to a request for comment.
Although authorities haven’t publicly said how many women were victimized by the “driving school” network, they have said their investigation is ongoing, meaning there could be further arrests and additional victims. Gebhard’s client, for example, only learned she had been sexually assaulted after investigators discovered video footage.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
On Wednesday, Zhiting S., a 32-year-old trained medic, was convicted of being an accessory to rape, among other charges, and sentenced to five years in prison. The defense plans to appeal the verdict.
The Berlin state court found that in the chats, Zhiting S. had pointed to a particular sedative before an assault by the man convicted in Frankfurt, though he wasn’t alone in offering such advice.
Zhiting S. also was convicted on three charges of sexual coercion related to alleged abuse of his partner in China. Video recordings led investigators to those crimes.
Defense attorney Ehssan Khazaeli said earlier that his client has admitted being part of a chat group but did not offer any significant advice.
Wu reported from Bangkok. Associated Press reporters Geir Moulson and Fanny Brodersen in Berlin, Molly Quell and Mike Corder in The Hague, Netherlands, Jaimie Ding in Los Angeles and Jill Lawless in London contributed to this report.

item.title: 法官下令在特朗普性虐待和诽谤案中向 E. Jean Carroll 支付 580 万美元；特朗普上诉
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——作者周三，一名联邦法官裁定，陪审团发现唐纳德·特朗普总统对简·卡罗尔进行性虐待和诽谤，让·卡罗尔可以领取由托管机构保管的 580 万美元。特朗普的律师立即要求法院在上诉期间阻止付款。
总统已经把钱存入一个账户。美国最高法院最近维持了 2023 年民事判决，为法官刘易斯·A·卡普兰 (Lewis A. Kaplan) 释放这笔钱扫清了道路。最初的 500 万美元奖金随着利息的增加而增加。
陪审团发现，特朗普于 1996 年在曼哈顿一家豪华百货公司的更衣室里袭击了卡罗尔，并在卡罗尔在 2019 年的回忆录中公开描述此事后对她进行了诽谤，当时特朗普正担任总统的第一个任期。特朗普称她的指控不实，并在接受采访时表示“她不是我喜欢的类型”。
特朗普的律师周三表示，他们将继续上诉，并指责他的政治对手利用法律体系来对付他。他们希望美国第二巡回上诉法院停止付款。卡罗尔的律师没有立即回应置评请求。
在卡罗尔作证说他们在百货公司的调情和友好偶遇变成了暴力之后，陪审团在特朗普没有参加的审判中做出了裁决。特朗普坚称他从来不认识现年 82 岁的卡罗尔，卡罗尔曾是一名咨询专栏作家。他指责她试图以他的利益为代价出售书籍，并且有政治动机。
在纽约修改法律，为性虐待幸存者提供新的机会起诉遥远过去发生的袭击事件后，卡罗尔起诉了特朗普。
特朗普还对曼哈顿另一个陪审团在 2024 年审判后给予卡罗尔 8300 万美元的诽谤赔偿提出上诉，其中特朗普曾短暂作证。
在那次审判中，卡普兰要求陪审团接受前一个陪审团的调查结果，并且只确定特朗普因卡罗尔在担任总统期间对卡罗尔发表的评论而欠卡罗尔多少钱（如果有的话）。
特朗普的律师抱怨说，法官在制定损害赔偿审判规则时，禁止特朗普和他的辩护团队告诉陪审团，与卡罗尔的遭遇从未发生过。
当第二巡回法院拒绝让所有法官重新审理对 8300 万美元赔偿金的上诉时，巡回法官丹尼·钦 (Denny Chin) 写道，特朗普多年来多次表示，卡罗尔为了政治和经济利益而撒谎，并暗示她太没有吸引力，特朗普不应该对她进行性侵犯。
钦说：“由于特朗普的言论，卡罗尔受到骚扰和羞辱，受到死亡威胁，多年来一直担心自己的人身安全。”“特朗普毫无悔意，在两次联邦审判期间和之后继续攻击卡罗尔，甚至在卡罗尔一号审判两天后宣布，他将继续诽谤她‘一千次’。”

item.title: 威斯康星州前法官因阻碍 ICE 逮捕墨西哥移民而免于入狱
item.description:
密尔沃基（美联社）——周三，前威斯康星州法官汉娜·杜甘（Hannah Dugan）因在美国移民和海关执法局人员试图在法院走廊逮捕一名墨西哥被告时将一名墨西哥被告从陪审团室门口带入监狱而被保释。
美国地区法官林恩·阿德尔曼 (Lynn Adelman) 对她处以 5,000 美元罚款，称此案是一个原本善良的人对这个国家的移民政策感到不满，却在当下做出了错误的决定。
67 岁的杜根于 12 月被判犯有妨碍司法罪重罪。她的律师在审判期间辩称，唐纳德·特朗普总统的政府试图“镇压”杜根，以确保司法部门遵守 ICE 针对移民出席法庭听证会的战略。
杜根于一月份辞去了担任了九年的密尔沃基县巡回法官一职，因为共和党州议员威胁要弹劾她，称她为激进法官。她在辞职信中表示，她的起诉威胁到“我们司法机构的独立性”。美国共和党众议员汤姆·蒂芙尼（Tom Tiffany）是特朗普的忠实拥护者，竞选威斯康星州州长，他曾敦促当局“将她关起来”。
美国威斯康星州东区检察官办公室在周三的一份声明中强调，去年 12 月陪审团的裁决和阿德尔曼的判决反映了杜根滥用职权阻碍执法人员的行为。
“执法人员需要能够以对他们、公众和他们试图拘留的个人最安全的方式履行其合法职责，”美国第一助理检察官布拉德·希梅尔说。“杜根的鲁莽和非法行为干扰了这一目标，并给所有相关人员带来了不必要的风险。为此，需要产生严重的后果。”
杜根在法庭上发表讲话，称她作为一名法官已尽力而为，她在 2025 年 4 月那天的行为并非恶意，而是为了维护“法庭的礼仪和安全”。
杜根说：“我被塑造成既是一个恶棍，又是一个英雄。我两者都不是。”她补充说，她正在努力做好自己的工作。她说，由于她和她的家人受到威胁，她不得不退出公共生活。
美国检察官执行助理理查德·弗罗林承认杜根经历了附带后果，但表示“法官不能选择无视法律”。
法官表示，杜根失去了工作，现在被判重罪，并受到威胁，迫使她搬家并停止参​​加社区活动。他还指出，杜根的行为并没有阻止 ICE 特工在法院外逮捕被告。
阿德尔曼表示：“这一定罪证明没有人可以凌驾于法律之上。”
检察官在量刑备忘录中辩称，杜根违反了她作为法官的誓言，并将执法部门和公众置于危险之中。
“法官被赋予极大的自由裁量权，但有一条他们不能跨越的界限，”弗罗林写道。“被告越过了那条线。”
杜根的律师辩称，她已经“受到足够的惩罚”，不应被判处超出她在联邦拘留期间的任何监禁时间。
联邦量刑指南要求监禁 15 至 21 个月，但这位由比尔·克林顿总统于 1997 年任命的法官不受这些指南的约束。检察官没有建议判处刑罚，但弗罗林写道，“这是一项严重的罪行，应该判处相应的严重刑罚。”
杰森·卢扎克律师在宣判后表示，他们仍会对杜根的妨碍司法重罪定罪提出上诉。陪审员在审判中宣判她无罪，罪名是隐匿他人以防止被捕，属于轻罪。
2025 年 4 月 18 日，移民官员在得知 31 岁的爱德华多·弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯 (Eduardo Flores-Ruiz) 非法入境美国并计划在杜根 (Dugan) 出席州殴打案听证会后前往密尔沃基县法院。
杜根在法庭外与特工对峙，并指示他们前往首席法官办公室，称他们的行政令不足以逮捕弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯。她的律师表示，在审判期间，她遵守了要求法院工作人员向其主管报告任何移民代理的协议。
特工离开后，她带领弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯和他的律师走出私人陪审团大门。特工在走廊里发现了弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯，跟踪他到外面，并在一番追逐后逮捕了他。一周后，联邦调查局特工在法院逮捕了杜根，并给她戴上手铐。
美联社撰稿人包括爱荷华州得梅因的 Hannah Fingerhut。鲍尔从威斯康星州麦迪逊报道。

item.title: 肯塔基州州长安迪·贝希尔要求参议员米奇·麦康奈尔公开更新他的病情
item.description:
Kentucky Gov.Andy Beshearis directly askingSen. Mitch McConnell, the state’s most powerful figure in Congress, to disclose more about his condition after three weeks of silence from the 84-year-old since he was hospitalized in Washington.
The letter released Wednesday from Beshear, a Democrat who is considered a potential presidential candidate in 2028, to the former Senate Republican leader says, “Kentuckians have grown increasingly concerned about the current state of your health and well-being, and ability to hold office.”
McConnell, whose physical condition has visibly declined in recent years, was hospitalized June 14. He has not released a public statement, photos or videos since. Aides have disclosed nothing specific about his condition, other than to say last week that McConnell “continues to improve, and is working closely with his staff on Kentucky and Senate matters while the Senate is out of session.”
That lack of detail has fueled rampant speculation about his prognosis and whether he will return to the Senate when it reconvenes next week. The firestorm was enough that Republican Senate leaders, including Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D. and Wyoming Sen. John Barrasso, on Tuesdaymade public statementssaying they had talked to McConnell and he was alert and discussing current events.
Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One on Wednesday evening, as he returned home following the NATO summit in Turkey, Trump was asked about McConnell but said the pair had not spoken.
“I have no idea how he’s doing,” Trump said.
McConnell isretiring at the end of his termin January, and the campaign to elect his successor already is underway. Kentucky’s Senate succession law, which Republican legislators have twice changed during Beshear’s tenure, does not give the governor a role in picking a temporary successor should McConnell’s seat become vacant before his term ends.
Under the latest change in 2024, Beshear would call a special election if the seat became vacant. The winner of that election could take office after the result is certified. The general election winner would be sworn in as part of the new Congress in January. But there are unresolved questions about the timing of a special election under the untested law and the possibility the seat could remain vacant until January.
Beshear ended the letter by wishing McConnell “a safe and speedy recovery.”
A look at what an absence from the Senate or a vacancy could mean.
There is not much, if anything, that Beshear, Kentucky lawmakers or the Senate could do if McConnell remains in office but is unable to perform his duties between now and when the current Congress expires in January.
Senate rules do not allow proxy voting. But there have been extended Senate absences before, and the chamber has continued its business with however many senators are in attendance. Republicans currently hold a 53-47 advantage. Without McConnell, that means a maximum of 52 Republican votes are available.
McConnell had been among the senators blocking war powers resolutions that seek to limit President Donald Trump’s military options in Iran. Without him, the administration has less of a buffer. On the other hand, McConnell already had been among the Republicans refusing to support Trump’s sweeping elections law overhaul.
The 17th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution calls for Senate vacancies to be filled by popular elections. But it allows state legislatures to empower governors to appoint an interim senator to serve through those campaigns. Most states have taken this option, according to theCongressional Research Service.
Kentucky law allowed interim appointments until 2021, when McConnell and other state GOP leaders persuaded the Republican-run Legislature to make a change. They did not want Beshear to shift the partisan balance in Washington if he got the opportunity. They called for a committee of the previous senator’s state party to select three people from whom the governor could choose an interim senator. In this case, that would mean Beshear picking which Republican would fill a vacancy. Several states have this system.
Kentucky lawmakers changed the law again in 2024 to require a special election. The only role for the governor is to call that election.
Beshear vetoed the 2021 and 2024 changes but Republican lawmakers overrode him.
The 2024 law says Beshear “shall” issue a proclamation for a special vote but it does not say when he should make that proclamation or what the election date must be. Separate laws require certain minimum windows between a proclamation and the election date, but not necessarily a maximum window.
Some officials have argued that any vacancy after Aug. 3 would mean a special election concurrent with the general. They have even speculated that at some point, it would be impractical to have a special election at all given the regular election already taking place.
The Kentucky secretary of state’s office declined to speculate on a hypothetical time frame.
If a special election was needed, the simplest option would be to hold it at the same time as the regular general election.
For the full Senate term that begins in 2027, Republicans nominated U.S. Rep. Andy Barr and Democrats nominated former state lawmaker Charles Booker. Concurrent elections would be separate, requiring new nominations by the parties, though they could choose Barr and Booker. Regardless, in this scenario voters would be electing the immediate replacement and the full-term lawmaker on the same Election Day.
Multiple vacant House seats have been filled that way with little national attention.
Yes. The 2024 law has never been tested. If a vacancy occurred, there could be different interests between parties and even among Republicans about special election timing and whether to hold one at all. That could create any number of legal questions and disputes that have to be settled by the courts.
Beshear’s office did not immediately respond to an inquiry about how he interprets the law.
This story has been corrected to reflect that it depends on what the governor might do to determine whether there might be a special election if there was a Senate vacancy after Aug. 3 and whether the seat might remain vacant until January.

item.title: 特朗普在从土耳其回国途中乘坐的是旧空军一号，而不是卡塔尔赠送的新飞机
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — PresidentDonald Trumpflew partway home from aNATO summit in Turkeyon an old baby blue Air Force One plane instead of the new Qatari-gifted and retrofittedred, white and navy blue jethe arrived in, a surprise swap that came as the U.S. and Iranonce again began trading strikes.
Trump offered little clarity on the swap, instead saying he would fly on the legacy aircraft “for old time’s sake,” and indicating that both aircraft would make a previously unscheduled stop on the way back to the United States at Royal Air Force Mildenhall, a base used by U.S. troops.
The travel switch raised fresh security questions about the new aircraft that the U.S.spent $400 million to retrofit.Images of the Qatari-gifted jetcaptured since its unveiling showit is not equipped with some of the same missile detection and countermeasure systems as the older jets.
Trump boarded the new jet at Mildenhall, and it promptly took off for Joint Base Andrews outside Washington after he greeted service members stationed there.
“We just landed and met up with our new Air Force One, which was sent earlier to RAF Mildenhall, so we could show the wonderful Servicemembers, as per the entire Base’s request,” the Republican president said on social media. “They were very excited.”
He said stopping at Mildenhall “was on our way back to the States from Turkey, with virtually no deviation of flightpath.”
During the flight, Trump denied to the reporters accompanying him that security concerns involving Iran were a factor in flying two planes home. Asked if he was aware of any credible threats against Air Force One by Iran, Trump brushed off the question.
“I have a threat all the time. I’m No. 1 on their list,” he said, repeating comments from earlier Wednesday that he is one of Iran’s top assassination targets.
When the reporters asked Trump if he knew why they were asked to keep their window blinds closed during the flight from Turkey to the U.K. aboard the older Air Force One, Trump replied that it probably was due to the “sleazebags over here” — an apparent reference to Iran. He said he was not asked to close the window blinds in his compartment. Iran and Turkey share a border.
The swap was also announced less than a day after the U.S. military conducted aseries of large strikes in Iranin retaliation for its attacks on merchant shipping in the region, and before a new series of strikes on Wednesday. Iran shares a border with Turkey.
Trump first announced in a social media post that thegleaming new planehe had proudly shown off a day earlier would instead visit the U.K. base on the way home so military members could “tour the Aircraft.” Trump said he instead would be flying home in an older plane previously used as Air Force One.
When asked later during a news conference if security concerns had played a role in the switch, Trump didn’t directly answer but said that when it came to Iran, he was “No. 1 on the list for killing.”
When another reporter followed up, Trump said he’d be “going home by normal methods” while the new plane would be shown off to troops.
When asked if the missing countermeasures systems played a role in the jet being swapped out, the U.S. Air Force directed questions to the White House.
“The new Air Force One is a state-of-the-art aircraft that has been fitted with high-level security protocols that ensure the safety of the President and his staff,” spokesman Steven Cheung said in a statement. “As the President has said recently, there are many enemies of America who have their sights on him, and we use every tool at our disposal — including distraction and misdirection — to address those threats.”
Trump departed Turkey aboard one of the older Boeing VC-25As that have carried presidents for 3 1/2 decades. Consumer flight trackers were unable to monitor its transponder early in the flight after takeoff, suggesting it had been temporarily disabled by the crew -- a security measure used when ferrying the president to and from high-risk environments like war zones, not a major NATO ally hosting a long-scheduled summit.
Other world leaders’ flights departed with trackable transponders, including those from Germany and the U.K.
Theluxurious Boeing 747-800gifted by Qatar, that was modified to carry Trump, departed earlier Wednesday from Turkey and landed at RAF Mildenhall on Wednesday afternoon, flight trackers showed.
Iran has several missiles and drones in its inventory with enough range to make the roughly 800-mile (1,300-kilometer) flight from its own borders to Turkey, including some of its Shahed drones and Shahab ballistic missiles.
However, according to the Center for Strategic and International Studies,Iran does not possessweaponry that would be capable of effectively striking England at a range of roughly 2,500 miles (4,000 kilometers).
The U.S. Air Force, which oversees the running of the fleet of aircraft used by every president, had previously said that they had to prioritize making only some of the necessary upgrades and changes in order to deliver the Qatari jet — also known as the “bridge” aircraft — into service.
The Air Force argued that the rapid conversion of the jet was done “without accepting any risk regarding security, safety, or secure communications,” but did concede that “several highly complex engineering modifications required for the final (Air Force One aircraft) were intentionally excluded from the Bridge aircraft.”
Jeremiah Gertler, a senior analyst for Teal Group, an aviation and defense consulting firm, previously told The Associated Press that the absence of countermeasure systems, as well as a seemingly smaller number of communications antennas, suggested that the Qatari jet was better suited to only work as a domestic aircraft.
Trump’s first flight on the new Qatari jet wasto North Dakota last week.
The original Air Force One planes were built from scratch near the end of the Cold War and they were hardened against the effects of a nuclear blast and included a range of security features, such as anti-missile countermeasures and an onboard operating room.
The jets are also equipped with air-to-air refueling capabilities for contingencies, though it has never been utilized with a president on board.
The pair of Boeing jets that are currently being modified to act as the permanent upgrades to the Air Force One jets have been delayed, and are expected to be delivered in 2028.
Associated Press writers Zeke Miller and Darlene Superville contributed to this report.

item.title: 为什么世界杯比赛用球总是在比赛期间回到球场
item.description:
亚特兰大（美联社）——世界杯比赛用球在世界杯期间凭借其自身的实力成为明星。
该球因其嵌入式技术而成为头条新闻，并引起了球迷的疑问，包括：为什么每个人都希望迅速将球扔回球场？
这个问题更多是出于好奇，而不是对变革的强烈要求。
对于世界杯观众来说，接住比赛用球的兴奋感是短暂的，因为根据国际足联的政策，比赛工作人员在球场上等待球迷将球扔回去。
如果有幸拿到球，一些球迷会尝试与球快速自拍。比赛用球绝对是一件令人垂涎的纪念品，尤其是对于那些花费数千美元购买机票、酒店和 100 级门票的人来说。
“我的意思是，我花了多少钱，当我从体育场接住足球时，我绝对能够保留它，”英格兰球迷杰克·古德温说。“我攒钱买了一栋房子，花光了全部押金，才把我和父亲从伦敦带了出来。”
这是美国体育迷的传统——在一场比赛中看到棒球，你就会得到一份终生的纪念品。如果这是季后赛比赛中的球或击球手的里程碑时刻，则奖励。
“我们不知道，也不在乎，”一名刚果球迷在被问及是否知道为什么球迷不能把球踢进看台时说道。“我们来这里不是为了获奖，我们来这里是为了观察我们的国家。”
棒球迷们互相攀爬，跳过座位，寻找飞过来的球。大多数球迷在把球扔回去时都没有问题。
深入了解“为什么？”国际足联的政策如此严格，可能会很困难。国际足联没有回复请求对此事发表评论的电子邮件。然而，理论的范围从传统到经济问题，到球的技术，到比赛的流程。
“我认为从来没有一项政策可以让你保留球，与棒球不同，球是一种相当昂贵的设备，”足球历史学家查尔斯·卡顿说。“在过去，一场比赛中只有一个球并不罕见。就是这样。如果它进入了看台，你必须将它取回或归还，这样比赛才能继续。现在你已经让它们驻扎在球场各处了。”
球迷可以从授权供应商或临时摊位购买 FIFA 球，价格从 60 美元到 180 美元不等。目前还不清楚足球管理机构为比赛日用球支付了多少费用，不过国际足联似乎可以不惜一切代价在手头上多备一些球，以确保比赛不会因球驶入看台而被球迷抓住而中断。
然而，尽管美国职业棒球大联盟的球迷最终赢得了将球击入看台的权利，但多年来，足球界一直坚定地坚持着自己的期望。足球本身的演变只会增强国际足联恢复每一个球的兴趣。
今年的世界杯用球阿迪达斯 Trionda 是迄今为止最先进的。
手工缝制、皮革、32 片球的时代已经一去不复返了。
佐治亚理工学院教授贾德·雷迪说：“它们已经从皮革等天然材料演变为现在的聚合材料。”“这样做有几个原因。首先是成本，还有性能，因为聚氨酯不像皮革那样吸收水分。”
Trionda 是一款四片热压聚氨酯球，但最具决定性的特征可能是在内部，其中 500Hz 运动传感器将实时数据发送到视频助理裁判系统，以帮助官员做出决定，包括经常引起争议的越位判罚。
实时数据也会传送给比赛裁判以确定球是否越过球门线。
“世界杯用球内部有射频发射器，基本上可以用作 GPS，还有加速计，可以了解踢球的力量，”雷迪说。
Ready 的同事 Manos Tentzeris 教授表示，该传感器的准确度为 99.99%。与摄像头配合使用，它可以精确定位球场上任何物体的精确位置，小到鞋尖，这足以确定球员是否越位。
这项技术在体育场外就没那么有价值了。雷迪表示，该球依赖体育场基础设施与官员沟通，这使得电子设备在世界杯场馆外几乎无法使用。
球还必须在比赛前使用无线系统充电，类似于为智能手表充电。
因为这是一个过程，所以比赛用球似乎不太可能成为球迷的纪念品，但永远不要说永远不会——有一天它可能会发生。

item.title: 卡米拉女王访问温布尔登并与扮演她丈夫的演员一起参加
item.description:
伦敦（美联社）——周三，英国女王卡米拉按照惯例访问了温布尔登，一位曾经饰演过她丈夫的演员也来到了皇家包厢。
这是国王查理三世的妻子女王连续第四年在为期两周的锦标赛的第 10 天访问全英俱乐部。当她坐在座位上观看茉莉花·保利尼 (Jasmine Paolini) 和玛塔·科斯秋孔 (Marta Kostyukon) 在中央球场进行的女子四分之一决赛时，她穿着一件浅蓝色连衣裙。
皇家包厢中的其他知名面孔包括演员艾丽·范宁 (Elle Fanning) 和多米尼克·韦斯特 (Dominic West)，后者因在 Netflix 电视剧《王冠》中饰演查尔斯而获得艾美奖提名。
科斯秋克获胜后，卡米拉让接下来的两名球员——英国外卡选手阿瑟·费里和意大利选手弗拉维奥·科博利——在他们准备走进中心球场时上前与他们握手，这让他们感到惊讶。
比赛开始前，卡米拉会见了一名球童和一名球女，以及几位在全英俱乐部长期任职的工作人员。
威尔士王妃凯特是全英俱乐部的赞助人，她上周访问了温布尔登，在场外与排着著名队列的球迷会面，并坐在两届冠军安迪·穆雷旁边观看网球比赛。

item.title: 世界杯四分之一决赛：梅西、摩洛哥和欧洲6支球队。这并不罕见
item.description:
佛罗里达州迈阿密花园（美联社）——更多世界杯球队。同样的世界杯故事。
本届赛事共有 48 支队伍参加；现在已减少至八个，其中六个来自欧洲。除非摩洛哥在最后两周取得一系列令人惊讶的结果，否则冠军将来自欧洲或南美洲。
这项赛事已有近一个世纪的历史——首届世界杯于 1930 年举行——此前已举办过 22 届比赛。冠军：12 名来自欧洲，10 名来自南美，全球其他地区总计 0 名。
今年的四分之一决赛阵容：六名来自欧洲，一名来自南美，一名来自非洲。这并不完全是一系列违背历史的结果。
也就是说，似乎连一些欧洲最优秀的球员都对本届世界杯的顺利进行感到惊讶。
“我认为有些事情是不可能做的，”挪威球星埃尔林·哈兰在他的两个进球帮助球队击败巴西队进入四分之一决赛后说道，这是挪威队第一次在世界杯上走得这么远。“我想我错了。”
他错了。锦标赛主办方犯了一个不太好的错误。
今年的世界杯规模比以往任何时候都大，共有 48 支球队参赛，美国、墨西哥和加拿大都是共同主办国，北美队在今年的世界杯中三度突破。
这些球队甚至都没有进入四分之一决赛。
“我们需要克服下一个障碍，”美国队在 16 强赛中被比利时队淘汰后，美国球星克里斯蒂安·普利西奇在接受电视采访时说道，这场以 4-1 的不平衡失利表明北美队还有很长的路要走。“尝试竞争并击败世界上最好的选手，这是我们的下一步......我们还必须采取另一步。”
三位东道主均轻松晋级小组赛并晋级32强。美国队、墨西哥队和加拿大队在这些比赛中总共取得了 9 胜 2 平 1 负的战绩，总共比对手多进了 20 个进球。至少可以说，事情看起来很有希望。
英格兰3-2墨西哥淘汰美国这场从一开始就显得一边倒的比赛被比利时队击败，而加拿大队则以 3-0 输给了摩洛哥队。这三场比赛的综合数据：0-3-0 战绩，净胜球数为 7 个。
“水平，”法国足球传奇人物蒂埃里·亨利在美国队失利后在福克斯电视台担任分析师时说道。“世界杯的小组赛阶段是不同的。32强赛以前从未存在过。每个人都在32强赛中创造了历史。以前从未存在过……不幸的是，一个东道主、两个东道主、三个东道主出局了。这正是你在世界杯上不想要的。这对我来说很烦人。”
如果这让亨利烦恼，想象一下加拿大人、墨西哥人和美国人的感受。
自2014年哥斯达黎加队以来，中北美洲及加勒比海足球协会（CONCACAF）球队还没有进入过世界杯四分之一决赛。
在此之前，美国队在 2002 年世界杯上取得了自 1930 年获得第三名以来的最佳表现。加拿大队从未闯过 16 强。墨西哥队参加了过去九届世界杯；墨西哥队在过去 9 届世界杯上都参加过；而加拿大队则从未闯过 16 强。其中八次进入16强，但在此期间再也没有进一步发展。
“每个人都付出了一切，”墨西哥中场球员埃里克·利拉说。“但是，最终，这还不够。”
对于欧洲和南美以外的所有地方来说，这似乎是每四年一次的重复。
2002 年世界杯四分之一决赛的阵容有一定的多样性，欧洲、南美洲、亚洲、非洲和北美这五个联合会都派出了那一年的代表队。
这是一个罕见的现象。此后的六届世界杯，在 48 个四分之一决赛席位中，欧洲占据了 30 个，南美占据了 14 个，非洲占据了 3 个，北美占据了 1 个。
摩洛哥现在承载着非洲的希望，而非洲大陆从小组赛中脱颖而出，似乎已经做好了突破的准备。派出了10支球队参加比赛；9人进入32强。
然后，大多数球队的希望都因最后时刻失球而破灭。
科特迪瓦、南非和刚果都在第 86 分钟或更晚的时间里投出了决定性的进球，最终被淘汰出局。佛得角的神奇旅程在第111分钟被梅西和卫冕冠军阿根廷队自摆乌龙后结束。对于塞内加尔和埃及来说，结局尤其残酷——下半场末段双方都以2-0领先，随后分别以2-3的比分输给了比利时和阿根廷。
埃及队认为裁判的决定窃取了比赛的胜利。
“也许他们想让世界冠军留在比赛中，”埃及教练霍萨姆·哈桑说。“也许他们希望梅西留在比赛中。”
与此相反的是，像阿根廷这样的强国如何再次找到了出路。梅西的球队在过去六届世界杯中第五次进入四分之一决赛。
梅西表示：“在世界杯淘汰赛中，从0-2落后的情况下翻盘并不容易——尤其是考虑到现在的比赛情况，没有人会免费给你任何东西。”“但是感谢上帝，我们再次做到了。”

item.title: ABC 在“The View”和平等时间规则争议中反击 FCC 监管机构
item.description:
美国广播公司正在继续强烈反击联邦监管机构重新审视其热门脱口秀节目《The View》是否受到平等时间规则的质疑。
该网络指责特朗普政府试图在不断升级的争端中压制言论自由，并在周二公开的向联邦通信委员会提交的一份新文件中辩称，该问题在二十多年前就已由该委员会自行解决。
作为 FCC 审查流程的一部分，需要提交最新的 ABC 备案。它包括对委员会的“回复评论”，以支持该电视网要求做出一项声明性裁决的请愿书，即《观点》——一个结合了娱乐和政治采访的长期播出的早间节目，经常以批评唐纳德·特朗普总统的评论为特色——是一个真正的新闻节目。
ABC 引用了 2002 年 FCC 的一项决定，对《The View》进行了限定，这意味着它不受同等时间规则的约束。这些规则要求给予竞争公职的候选人平等的通话时间。
在 5 月份的一份文件中，美国广播公司同样指责特朗普政府试图通过重新提出有关“观点”的问题来冷却其受宪法保护的言论自由并阻碍公开的政治讨论。
这是美国媒体与特朗普政府之间法律领域内外更广泛争议的最新一起，争议焦点是记者认为总统对言论自由和媒体履行职责的能力的攻击。特朗普一直批评那些新闻报道与他的议程背道而驰的媒体。
他的联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔表示，他打算辩称“The View”不是所谓的真正的新闻节目。该问题可能会影响其他类似地结合娱乐和政治的节目。
在 5 月份提交文件后，FCC 就该问题开启了公众评议期，作为审查过程的一部分。ABC 在周二提交的文件中指出，公众已提交了超过 77,000 条评论，其中“不可否认的大多数”信息支持“观点”和言论自由。
新提交的文件称：“评论者的担忧是正确的。”“第一修正案不允许政府担任编辑的职位。然而，这就是委员会现在提议占据的席位……决定哪些广播节目有资格成为合法新闻，对于那些委员会认为有需要的节目，迫使他们将播出时间交给他们从未选择的嘉宾。”
报告称，关于《观点》的争议触及了一个更大的原则：“联邦监管机构是否可以推翻广播公司对采访对象的编辑判断——宪法将这一判断交给广播公司及其观众，而不是国家。”
美国广播公司还辩称，“自二十多年前委员会上次回答该问题以来，法律所关心的‘观点’没有任何变化……改变的不是该计划，而是其周围的政治气候。”
它声称卡尔的联邦通信委员会选择将注意力集中在白天和深夜的节目上，“被认为对现任政府不友好——而没有触及谈话广播的广阔前景，候选人通常会在没有对手的情况下出现。”
它说，这“不是公平的监管”。
FCC 发言人在给美联社的电子邮件中回应称 ABC 误导公众。
该发言人表示：“虽然 ABC 坚称《The View》是法律规定的‘善意新闻节目’，但 ABC 应该专注于遵守其公共利益义务，而不是误导公众。”
政府对《观点》的批评与对批评特朗普的深夜新闻主持人——尤其是美国广播公司的吉米·金梅尔的不满相呼应。唐纳德和梅拉尼娅·特朗普最近都呼吁美国广播公司解雇金梅尔，因为金梅尔在漫画中形容第一夫人“焕发着准寡妇的光芒”。基梅尔说，这个笑话是对这对夫妇年龄差异的轻微讽刺。
乔斯林·诺维克 (Jocelyn Noveck) 为美联社报道媒体和娱乐的交叉领域。

item.title: 《皮特》以 25 项艾美奖提名领跑，《黑客》以 24 项艾美奖提名打破喜剧片纪录
item.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) —“The Pitt”led all nominees with 25 in a dominant sophomore season, while“Hacks”got a graduation party with a record-setting 24 to lead all comedies in its final season asEmmy nominations were announced Wednesday.
The totals give HBO Max the top spot for both drama and comedy, with “Hacks” breaking the record for most nominations for a comedy series held jointly by“The Studio”last year and“The Bear”in 2024. HBO Max led all outlets with 122 overall nominations and has three shows up for both best drama and best comedy series.
The nominees announced for118 Primetime Emmy categoriesincluded the lateRob Reinerfor his guest acting on “The Bear,”Bad Bunny for his Super Bowl halftimeshow, and newlywed Taylor Swift for “The Eras Tour - The Final Show” concert special.
This combination of images shows promotional art for Emmy nominated shows for best comedy, top row from left, “Abbott Elementary,” “The Bear,” “Hacks,” and “Margo’s Got Money Troubles,” bottom row from left, “Nobody Wants This,” Only Murders in the Building,” “Shrinking,” and “Widow’s Bay.” (ABC/FX/HBO Max/Apple TV+/Netflix/Hulu/Apple TV+/Apple TV+ via AP)
Emmy voters love a departing show, and have loved the tension-between-comedy-generations “Hacks” since its first season, allowing it to run up the numbers as a fifth-year senior. StarJean Smart has won best actressin a comedy for all four previous seasons and it would be stunning if she didn’t claim a fifth.
AP correspondent Julie Walker reports on the Emmy nominations and which shows could be winners this year.
Her sidekick throughout the seriesHannah Einbinder, who last year broke through and won supporting actress in a comedy in her fourth nomination, got a fifth. Her castmate, the show’s co-creator Paul W. Downs, got three nominations, for acting, writing and producing.
The day-in-the-lifeemergency roomseries“The Pitt”was a rookie upstart last year with big wins including best drama series, best actor for Noah Wyle and best supporting actress for Katherine LaNasa.
Already a beloved veteran show, it owned this year’s acting categories. Wyle was nominated again for best actor (along with nods for his directing and producing) as was LaNasa. Taylor Dearden, Fiona Dourif and Sepideh Moafi also got nominations, with “The Pitt” taking four of the seven supporting actress spots and three supporting actor slots.
In an era when major Emmy contenders — like 2025’s top drama nominee“Severance”— take years off between seasons,“The Pitt” came right backfor another round. And its claim on the acting categories was helped by the between-seasons absence of“The White Lotus,”and its elite ensembles.
This image released by HBO shows Jean Smart in a scene from “Hacks.” (HBO via AP)
Two new shows from Apple TV, the one-woman-against-the-hivemind drama“Pluribus”and the horror comedy“Widow’s Bay,”both scored big in their first seasons.
“Pluribus,”from “Breaking Bad” and “Better Call Saul” creator Vince Gilligan, got 18 nods. They included best actress in a drama for its only lead cast member Rhea Seahorn, considered the favorite to win.
“Widow’s Bay” got 19, including best actor for star Matthew Rhys, who was also nominated as a producer and for his acting in the limited series “The Beast in Me.”
Two other Apple TV shows are also up for best comedy,“Margo’s Got Money Troubles,”whose stars Elle Fanning and Michelle Pfeiffer got acting nominations, and “Shrinking.”
The “Shrinking” nominations included best supporting actor in a comedy for Harrison Ford. The force could be with Ford, with many prognosticators saying this will be the year the Hollywood legend finally wins an EGOT-tier award. His castmate Jason Segel is up for lead actor.
This image released by Apple TV shows Rhea Seehorn in a scene from “Pluribus.” (Apple TV via AP)
Many close observers also think this will be the year the perennially nominated Martin Short wins best actor in a comedy for “Only Murders in the Building.” He was nominated for three trophies, including nods for his producing of “Only Murders in the Building” and hosting “The Match Game.” A Netflix documentary about him, “Marty, Life is Short,” also got two nominations.
Jason Bateman got four nominations, for his performing and producing on both “Black Rabbit” and “DTF St. Louis.”
ABCs“Abbott Elementary”remained a rare bright spot for the traditional broadcast networks that have been marginalized in top categories by cable and streaming shows. Creator, star and two-time Emmy winner Quinta Brunson was nominated for three more, and the show got seven.
This image released by Apple TV+ shows Harrison Ford, left, and Michael J. Fox in a scene from “Shrinking.” (Apple TV+ via AP)
The vast range of Emmy categories brings unexpected stars and beloved figures into the mix.
Rob Reiner,who was killed along with wifeMichele Singer Reinerin December, has a chance at his third Emmy. He was nominated for guest actor for “The Bear,” about 50 years after winning two for his acting on “All in the Family.”
Five-time Emmy winner Michael J. Fox, also got a nomination in the same category for playing a patient with Parkinson’s disease, which the actor was diagnosed with in the 1990s, on “Shrinking.”
The Super Bowl halftime show is nearly always nominated, but this year’s featuringBad Bunnyon NBC went above and beyond with nine nominations.
Swift’s “Eras Tour” concert special got five nominations days after hermarriage to Travis Kelce, one of them for the singing superstar herself as producer. She has one previous Emmy, for interactive media in 2015, to go with her 14 Grammys.
And after a year of targeting from the Trump administration for late-night hosts and their shows, the now-off-the-air“Late Show With Stephen Colbert”got nine nominations and“Jimmy Kimmel Live!”got six.
This combination of images shows promotional art for Emmy nominees for best limited or anthology series, from left. “All Her Fault,” “The Beast in Me,” “Beef,” “DTF St. Louis,” and “Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette.” (Peacock/Netflix/Netflix/HBO Max/FX-Hulu via AP)
Netflix’s “Beef”was tops in the limited or anthology series categories with 16 nominations. “Beef” had a dominant first season in 2023, and the anthology’s all-new grudge holders, Carey Mulligan, Oscar Isaac and Charles Melton, all got nominations.
“Euphoria” made its Emmys return after a long absence and got seven nominations. Zendaya, who won best actress in a drama for the first two seasons in 2020 and 2022, got a nod for the recently aired third season.
Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller announced the nominees at the Television Academy in Los Angeles. The 78thPrimetime Emmy Awards, airing on NBC, will be held Sept. 14. Mariska Hargitay, the longtime star of NBC’s “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit,” will host and is a double nominee for directing and producing the documentary “My Mom Jayne.”

item.title: 电影评论：真人版《莫阿娜》是动画经典的无生命复制品
item.description:
不管你对它们有什么看法，但迪士尼真人翻拍至少给了我们一个选择。您更愿意看到哪一个？一部充满活力、翱翔天际的动画版《莫阿娜》，还是一部由道恩·强森和法比奥头发的无目的翻拍版？
海是广阔的，我的朋友们。然而，波利尼西亚旅行公主莫阿娜自从 10 年前首次出现在我们的屏幕上以来，似乎一直被困在游泳圈中。她是迪士尼最伟大的英雄之一。这部原版电影由手绘大师罗恩·克莱门茨和约翰·马斯克制作电脑动画，并在林-曼努尔·米兰达充满活力的歌曲的推动下，代表了本世纪迪士尼动画的高度。
但从那时起，水就变得汹涌澎湃。《海洋奇缘 2》最初计划作为一部流媒体剧集，但几乎没有对故事进行扩展。而新的真人翻拍版则做得更少。无论票房收入如何，很少有真人翻拍的影片能够获得荣耀。但由《汉密尔顿》老手托马斯·凯尔执导的《莫阿娜》感觉特别不合时宜，只不过是把卡通想象换成了真人版，可悲的是。死气沉沉的结果非常接近《周六夜现场》的戏仿或《蜘蛛侠宇宙》电影中那些开玩笑的平行宇宙之一。
新的《莫阿娜》通常是对原版进行逐个镜头、逐个音符的翻拍，其中加入了一些新材料。这在某种程度上是可以理解的。歌曲还是很棒的。扮演莫阿娜的凯瑟琳·拉加艾亚 (Catherine Laga'aia) 歌声优美。数字化的海洋更加蔚蓝。
但所有的创造力都已消失殆尽。动画的一大优点是它可以通过弹指完成任何事情。你可以感觉到想象力在发挥作用。原版《莫阿纳》的辉煌之处在于其丰富的波利尼西亚联系和卡通乐趣：半神毛伊（约翰逊饰）傻乎乎的变形，巨蟹塔马托亚（杰梅因·克莱门特饰）的脚趾轻敲动作，海洋将莫阿纳带回船上的方式。
让这些东西成为现实不仅仅是格式上的更新；它耗尽了他们活泼的灵魂。结果看起来更像是戏剧表演，而不是真正的艺术创作。我敢打赌，没有人在看过第一部《莫阿纳》中的小椰子海盗后对自己说：“我需要一个看起来更真实的卡卡莫拉。”
真人版更新的最大好处在于再现了莫阿娜的原生莫图努伊。如果说原版《莫阿娜》是为了向波利尼西亚文化致敬而制作的，那么真人的存在则让这一点变得更加切实。汤加血统的新西兰演员约翰·图伊 (John Tui) 尤其擅长饰演莫阿娜的首席父亲。
但从动画到真人的跨越往往会带来损失。对于注定要成为卡通的角色来说尤其如此，比如公鸡黑黑。塔马托亚的情况就是令人痛苦，他的“So Shiny”曲调曾经是不可否认的亮点，但现在可能是电影中最尴尬的场景。
这一切都不是拉加艾亚的错，她生动的表演是影片前进动力的主要来源。毛伊——自夸、狡猾的滑稽、不可避免的英雄——是约翰逊最适合的角色之一。因此，令人惊讶的是，他在这里的表演很难与动画毛伊岛的神韵相媲美。最近越来越多地被戏剧角色所吸引的约翰逊可能已经不再是一个原本是幻想的角色，而假发不足以掩盖这种差异。
随着克里斯托弗·诺兰的《奥德赛》即将登陆影院，对于有关诸神和归来的航海神话来说，这是一个异常好的时机。没有人知道这个系列会走多远——第三部动画电影正在开发中。但莫阿纳越来越迫切需要一些新的水域来探索。
华特迪士尼公司周四在影院上映的《莫阿娜》因动作/危险、一些恐怖的画面、粗鲁的幽默和简短的主题元素而被美国电影协会评为 PG 级。运行时间：115分钟。四颗星中的一颗半。

item.title: 特朗普表示与伊朗的停火“结束”后，油价上涨，全球股市下跌
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——周三，在美国总统唐纳德·特朗普对与伊朗的临时休战表示怀疑后，全球油价上涨，股市下跌，交易不稳定。
在特朗普表示停火协议“结束”后，标准普尔 500 指数一度下跌 1.1%，但在特朗普表示最近的战斗并不意味着恢复全面战争后，该指数将跌幅缩减至 0.3%。这些是他关于战争将会发生什么的最新混乱信息，这场战争可能会加剧世界的通货膨胀。
道琼斯工业平均指数下跌 576 点，跌幅 1.1%，纳斯达克综合指数在收复早盘跌幅后上涨 0.2%。
石油市场的走势更为强劲，每桶布伦特原油价格上涨 5.2%，至 78.02 美元，并一度突破 80 美元。
这仍低于战争初期的峰值，当时交易最活跃的合约价格达到近 120 美元。但油价的上涨令人不安，因为油价刚刚回落至战前水平。
令人担忧的是，战争的持续将封锁霍尔木兹海峡，阻止从波斯湾向世界各地的客户运送原油。这可能会加剧通货膨胀，经济学家预计通货膨胀会随着油价上涨而缓解，进而迫使美联储和其他央行加息。
较高的利率可以抑制通货膨胀，但也会减缓经济发展并损害各种投资的价格。
在华尔街，房地产行业公司的股票领跌。他们担心债券市场的国债收益率上升将意味着抵押贷款利率上升并令整个行业降温，这让他们感到受伤。
销售台面、窗户和其他建筑用品的 Builders FirstSource 股价下跌 5.4%。住宅建筑商 PulteGroup 下跌 5.4%，D.R.霍顿下跌4.6%。
燃油费用高的公司也陷入困境。美国航空 (American Airlines) 下跌 4%，邮轮运营商嘉年华 (Carnival) 下跌 3.9%。
人工智能行业一些有影响力的股票的稳定有助于抵消这些损失。最近几周，他们一直承受着压力，担心自己的价格过高，人工智能可能无法产生足够的生产力和利润，使芯片和数据中心的所有投资都值得。
它们的波动对华尔街影响很大，因为人工智能股票已经成长为美国市场最大的股票之一，这使得它们的走势对标准普尔 500 指数的影响比其他股票更大。
例如，英伟达股价上涨 3.7%，是推动标准普尔 500 指数上涨的最强劲力量，因为它是华尔街最大的股票。
紧随其后的是博通 (Broadcom)，在苹果宣布博通将为其产品设计和生产定制组件的多年承诺后，博通股价上涨 4.8%。苹果表示，该协议的价值可能超过 300 亿美元。
总而言之，标准普尔 500 指数下跌 21.14 点至 7,482.71 点。道琼斯工业平均指数下跌 576.76 点至 52,348.39 点，纳斯达克综合指数上涨 51.96 点至 25,870.65 点。
在债券市场，国债收益率随着油价上涨而上涨。10 年期国债收益率短暂升至 4.60% 附近，然后回落至 4.57%。这比周二晚间的 4.55% 以及与伊朗战争开始前的 3.97% 有所上升。
在海外股市，在特朗普就停火状态表示“对我来说，我认为一切都结束了”后，欧洲股市大幅下跌。他补充说，美国代表可以继续谈判，“但我认为他们在浪费时间。”德国DAX指数下跌2.2%，法国CAC 40指数下跌2.2%。
在亚洲，韩国综合股价指数下跌 5.3%，并在对主导市场的人工智能股票的担忧和乐观情绪中继续大幅波动。
香港恒生指数表现异常，上涨 3%。中国人工智能初创公司智普（又名“Z.ai”，交易名称为“知识图谱科技”）在那里交易的股票上涨了 13.4%。
该公司一月份在香港上市后，“基石”投资者的六个月禁售期将于本周到期。中央人民广播电台周二晚间报道称，尽管此前担心禁售期到期可能引发抛售，但近 70% 的智浦基石投资者仍决心继续持有。
自上市以来，智浦股价已上涨逾1300%。
美联社商业作家 Matt Ott、Chan Ho-him 和 Elaine Kurtenbach 对本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: Edmunds 表示，价格低于 15,000 美元的最二手轿车
item.description:
根据Edmunds的数据，一辆3年二手车的平均交易价格超过3万美元。与购买新产品相比，这是一个很大的折扣，但对于很多人来说，它仍然可能在经济上遥不可及。幸运的是，花大约一半的钱仍然可以买到一辆兼具舒适性、可靠性、燃油经济性和现代技术的汽车。
Edmunds 专家已经确定了他们建议您重点关注的四款小型轿车和一款中型轿车。每辆车都具有具有竞争力的 Edmunds 评分、二手市场上的广泛可用性以及来自 RepairPal（数据驱动可靠性评级的独立来源）的至少 4 分（满分 5 分）的可靠性评分。我们还注意到您可以在 Carvana 和 CarMax 等全国零售商处找到符合我们 15,000 美元价格上限的最新车型。
思域作为小型轿车中长期最受欢迎的地位是当之无愧的。它提供出色的燃油经济性和强劲的加速性能，而其悬架调校则在乘坐质量和运动能力之间实现了近乎完美的平衡。尽管思域的中央触摸屏有时使用起来很慢而且烦人，但思域宽敞的后座和紧凑的结构给人留下了深刻的印象。
寻找：较旧的思域很容易落入我们的价格上限，但如果可以的话，目标是购买 2016 款思域。它今年进行了全面重新设计，具有新的造型、更宽敞的内部空间和新的可选涡轮增压发动机，可提供充足的动力和高英里/加仑。
起亚的 Forte 是该品牌的小型轿车，直到几年前才出售，后来起亚用 K4 取代了它。Forte 鲜明的造型、安静的内饰以及丰富的可用功能使其感觉像是一辆比其价格所暗示的更精致的小型轿车。它还具有良好的燃油经济性，并具有宽敞且易于装载的后备箱。Forte 的乘坐质量可能有点太硬，颠簸时不舒服，但总的来说，对于二手轿车来说，它是一个明智的选择。
寻找：最后一代 Forte 于 2019 车型年首次亮相。从今年到 2021 年左右的任何 Forte 都应该很容易以低于 15,000 美元的价格找到。在 Forte 的可用装饰级别中，EX 值得关注，因为它配备了高级功能，例如合成皮革内饰以及加热和通风前排座椅。
虽然这一组中的大多数轿车要么根本不运动，要么只是装饰得恰到好处，但马自达 3 确实具有驾驶乐趣。即使性能不是您的首要任务，这种响应能力在紧急演习期间也可能是宝贵的资产。马自达3锐利的外观设计也颇具风格，而其内饰质量超出预期，其碰撞测试成绩使其跻身同级车之首。
寻找：第三代马自达 3 于 2014 年首次亮相，并于 2018 年生产。买家应该不难从该生产的后期中找到可靠的选择。2018 Touring 车型提供了一系列符合价格的标准功能，并配备了比低配车型更强大的发动机。
丰田卡罗拉是紧凑型轿车类别中长期运行的主打车型，以其提供简洁的运输和较低的运行成本而闻名。卡罗拉因其舒适性和一系列吸引人的先进驾驶员辅助功能而获得高分。它不像一些竞争对手那么宽敞，而且缓慢的加速使乐趣因素相对较低，但除此之外，卡罗拉是一个明智的选择。
寻找：丰田在 2013 年推出了第 11 代 Corolla。您应该能够以低于 15,000 美元的价格购买 2015 至 2017 年的 Corolla。值得注意的是，2017 款 Corolla 的特点包括改进的前端造型、新的 LED 头灯和升级的内饰。
如果是本田雅阁或丰田凯美瑞等热门车型，找到一辆车龄不到 10 年且价格低于 15,000 美元的二手中型轿车可能会很困难。但这正是雪佛兰迈锐宝的吸引力所在。作为该组中唯一的中型轿车，马里布为乘客及其货物提供了比此列表中的其他轿车更多的空间，但它仍然提供经过验证的可靠性和强大的整体价值。
寻找：当前一代 Malibu 于 2016 年上市。今年或更新的任何 Malibu 都是不错的选择。如果您能找到一款，那么 2019 款 Malibu 值得一看，因为它经过修改的造型和更新的信息娱乐技术。
这些车型证明您不需要花大钱就能获得一辆可靠且装备精良的轿车。从风格和技术到乘坐质量和效率，真正的决定取决于对您来说最重要的事情。每一种都有一定的优势，但它们都物有所值。
这个故事是由汽车网站埃德蒙兹向美联社提供的。Bradley Iger 是 Edmunds 的撰稿人。
